channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian drone strike kills one in Russian-annexed Crimea
item.description: A Ukrainian drone struck a train in Crimea, killing its assistant driver and injuring the driver, according to Russian-installed governor Sergei Aksyonov. The attack is part of ongoing Ukrainian strikes on Russian logistics.

item.title: 📉 South Korea KOSPI crashes over 8% as Fed fears spark tech rout
item.description: South Korea stock benchmark plunged over 8%, tripping circuit breakers, after robust US jobs data lifted bets on a Federal Reserve rate hike and unleashed a selloff in tech-heavy markets that had powered the AI rally.

item.title: 🇨🇺 UN says children dying in Cuba due to strict US sanctions
item.description: Tighter US sanctions on Cuba have led to a spike in infant mortality and plummeting survival rates for child cancer patients, according to a UN report highlighting humanitarian consequences of the economic measures.

item.title: 🏛️ Trump administration seeks to revoke US citizenship of 17 immigrants
item.description: The push to denaturalize more immigrants is the latest sign that the administration is setting its sights on the legal immigration system, expanding efforts beyond undocumented immigrants.

item.title: ⚖️ Federal judge rules Trump 100,000 dollar H-1B visa fee unlawful
item.description: A federal judge struck down the 100,000 dollar fee President Donald Trump ordered for H-1B visa applications, providing a reprieve for highly skilled worker immigration.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Sanctions IRGC Navy Unit Over Strait of Hormuz Blockade
item.description: The European Union imposed sanctions on two Iranian individuals and an IRGC naval unit for restricting maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, implementing asset freezes and travel bans.

item.title: 🇫🇷 French Fighter Jet Shoots Down Drone in Latvian Airspace During NATO Mission
item.description: A French Rafale fighter on NATO air patrol shot down a drone that entered Latvian airspace, marking what appears to be the first such engagement in Europe and underscoring growing drone threats near Russian borders.

item.title: 🚢 Houthis Ban Israeli Shipping From Red Sea Amid Iran-Israel Conflict
item.description: Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthis announced a ban on Israeli maritime navigation in the Red Sea, escalating threats to global shipping routes as the broader Iran-Israel conflict intensifies.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Foils Russian Plot to Assassinate Senior Military Intelligence Official
item.description: Ukrainian authorities announced they had thwarted a Russian plan to assassinate a senior military intelligence official, according to police statements amid ongoing tensions between the two countries.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Blames US for Latest Exchanges of Fire with Israel
item.description: Iran warned that overnight exchanges of fire with Israel will worsen the already chaotic diplomatic process with the United States. Tehran said it was exchanging messages with Washington in an atmosphere of extreme suspicion.

item.title: 🕊️ Trump Says Israel and Iran Seeking Immediate Ceasefire
item.description: US President Donald Trump stated both Israel and Iran are looking to do an immediate ceasefire, and that final negotiations on peace are proceeding. The announcement came as both sides halted strikes.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Xi Visits North Korea to Reassert Influence as Pyongyang Tilts Toward Moscow
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping made a rare visit to Pyongyang to deepen strategic cooperation with North Korea, seeking to remind Kim Jong Un that China remains the senior partner in their alliance amid growing North Korea-Russia ties.

item.title: 🇵🇱 Poland Seeks Dialogue with Ukraine Over Army Unit Row
item.description: Poland called for solidarity and talks with Ukraine after diplomatic relations deteriorated over the naming of a Ukrainian army unit for nationalist insurgents who massacred Poles during World War Two.

item.title: 🏛️ Republican Senators Warn Key US Surveillance Program May Lapse
item.description: Senators warned that a critical US surveillance authority could expire this week after bipartisan opposition to President Trump's intelligence pick. The program under debate allows warrantless collection of foreign communications.

item.title: 🛑 Israel and Iran Halt Strikes as Trump Pushes for Ceasefire
item.description: Iran and Israel paused attacks on each other after President Trump urged both sides to "stop shooting." Tehran warned it would resume strikes if Israel continues hitting Hezbollah in Lebanon.

item.title: 🛢️ Iran to Charge Transit Fees for Ships Passing Through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran's ambassador to Moscow announced the strategic waterway will remain open but under new conditions set by Iran and Oman, including transit fees—a move that could reshape global energy shipping.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇱🇻⚔️ French jet on NATO mission shoots down drone in Latvian airspace
item.description: A French Rafale fighter jet shot down a drone that entered NATO member Latvia's airspace from Russia, the latest in a series of security incidents along Europe's eastern border.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇦🇲⚠️ Russia warns Armenia of new trade curbs after pro-Western election
item.description: Russia signaled further restrictions on Armenian imports days after PM Pashinyan's election victory reinforced the country's pivot away from Moscow toward the West.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇳🇮 US imposes visa restrictions on Nicaraguans over indigenous leader death
item.description: The United States imposed additional visa restrictions on more than 100 Nicaraguan officials and their families over the death of indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera.

item.title: 🇰🇷🇰🇵 South Korea's Lee Jae Myung stresses pragmatic North Korea approach
item.description: President Lee Jae Myung said engagement through diplomacy must continue despite inter-Korean tensions, emphasizing a steady hand in dealing with Pyongyang.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇱🇻 French NATO jet shoots down drone in Latvian airspace
item.description: A French Rafale on NATO duty downed a drone that entered from Russia, the latest security incident along Europe's eastern border as Ukraine war intensifies.

item.title: ⛪🇪🇸 Pope Leo warns Spain's parliament world is in profound crisis
item.description: In the first papal address to Spain's legislature, Pope Leo condemned war as a "painful defeat" and called for respect for migrants and international law.

item.title: 🇨🇳📦 China exports set for strong May on front-loaded orders and chip demand
item.description: Export growth likely strengthened as firms rushed orders before Gulf war energy impacts, while global semiconductor and AI component demand remained robust.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan-Administered Kashmir Clashes Kill 11 Ahead of Protest Rally
item.description: At least 11 people were killed and more than 70 injured in clashes between protesters and security forces in Pakistan-administered Kashmir ahead of a Tuesday rally called by the banned Joint Awami Action Committee.

item.title: 🇺🇸📡 Amazon Signs Multi-Billion Dollar Deal With Corning for US Fiber Optics Production
item.description: Amazon announced a multi-billion dollar agreement with specialty glass maker Corning to boost US production of optical fiber and connectivity products used in data centers, creating 1,000 jobs in North Carolina.
